preflight.note: Stamped by `repopact new`; the work item was recorded before implementation began.

acceptance_criteria.text: A machine-readable inventory identifies every machine-enforceable SPEC rule and validation invariant, and an automated coverage gate fails when any such rule lacks a conformance fixture or when a declared fixture references an unknown rule.

Conformance fixtures cover provenance acceptance and rejection, status-directory mismatch, invalid semantic version, schema-invalid records, orphan work directories, disjoint active scopes, and canonical dashboard absence or drift, in addition to the existing cases.
Each fixture isolates its intended rule so a secondary dashboard or schema failure cannot mask the expected signal, and the runner reports both expected and unexpected violations deterministically.
CONFORMANCE.md states the complete coverage contract for alternative implementations, and governance validation, unit tests, SPEC generation, and the expanded conformance suite pass.
